name: Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail
name_original: Leungane y Mugarra desde Durango — circular trailrunning por el Parque Natural de Urkiola
description: Challenge yourself on the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail, a difficult 13.0-mile (21.0 km) jogging route with 3372 feet (1028 metres) of elevation
region_intro: Jogging around Durango offers access to a diverse landscape, characterized by the surrounding San Juan Mountains, the Animas River, and extensive trail networks. The region provides a variety of terrain, from paved riverside paths to challenging mountain trails through dense forests and alpine valleys. This makes Durango a destination for runners seeking both accessible urban routes and more demanding wilderness experiences. Best jogging routes around Durango The most popular jogging route is Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail, a 13.0 miles (21.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Muntsaratzeko dorrea loop from Durango, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.5 km) path with varied terrain and scenic views. Local runners also love the Larringan Windmill loop from Durango, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 26 minutes. Jogging around Durango is defined by the Animas River, the San Juan Mountains, and varied forest trails, offering options for easy runs, moderate loops, and challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Durango are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Durango's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Durango?
A: Durango offers a wide variety of running experiences, with around 50 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy, paved paths along the river to challenging mountain ascents in the San Juan Mountains.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Durango?
A: Yes, Durango has several options for easy runs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24661810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muntsaratzeko dorrea loop from Durango&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.2-mile path with varied terrain, suitable for those looking for a less strenuous experience. Additionally, the Animas River Trail, though not specifically listed in our routes, is a local favorite offering a flat, paved surface ideal for leisurely jogs.

Q: What are some challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Durango delivers with numerous difficult trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058628739/mikeldi-durangaldea-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 13.0-mile route with significant elevation gain. Other difficult options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24645404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larringan Windmill loop from Durango&lt;/a&gt; (7.7 miles) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Nevera de Neberondo – View Towards La Nevera loop from Durango&lt;/a&gt; (8.9 miles), both offering diverse landscapes and considerable climbs.

Q: Are there any loop running trails in the Durango area?
A: Absolutely. Many of Durango's running routes are designed as loops, allowing you to experience diverse scenery without retracing your steps. Popular loop options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24661810" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muntsaratzeko dorrea loop from Durango&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24645404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larringan Windmill loop from Durango&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features trails like the Dry Fork Loop, known for wildflowers.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on Durango's running trails?
A: Durango's running trails offer stunning and diverse scenic views. You can expect breathtaking river views along the Animas River Trail, towering peaks and dense pine forests in the San Juan National Forest, and picturesque reservoir vistas near Lake Nighthorse. Many routes also feature alpine valleys and vibrant wildflowers depending on the season.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by interesting landmarks or attractions?
A: While running, you can explore several notable points of interest. The region features significant summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anboto (1,331 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alluitz (1034m)&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/628313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urkiola Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/318367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Bizkaia Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Durango?
A: The running trails around Durango are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 runners have explored these varied terrains, often praising the diverse landscapes, from riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents, and the well-maintained network of trails.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Durango?
A: Many trails in the Durango area, especially those within the San Juan National Forest and other public lands, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations before heading out, as leash laws and restrictions can vary. The Animas River Trail is a popular choice for runners with dogs, provided they are on a leash.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Durango?
A: Durango offers excellent jogging conditions throughout much of the year due to its sunny days. Spring and fall provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery with wildflowers or changing leaves. Summer is also popular, especially for higher-elevation trails, while winter can offer unique snow running experiences on certain routes, though some trails may be less accessible.

Q: Are there any less crowded running trails for a more peaceful experience?
A: While the Animas River Trail can be popular, venturing into the San Juan National Forest or exploring trails around Lake Nighthorse often provides a more serene and less crowded running experience. Areas like Animas City Mountain, Grandview Ridge Trails, and Overend Mountain Park also offer extensive networks where you can find quieter paths away from the main thoroughfares.

Q: Where can I find parking for running trails in Durango?
A: Parking is generally available at various access points for Durango's trail systems. For the Animas River Trail, there are multiple parking lots along its length, including near downtown parks. Trailheads for routes in the San Juan National Forest and other mountain areas typically have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific trail information for the most convenient parking options.

Q: Can I find running routes with waterfalls in Durango?
A: Yes, the Durango area offers trails that feature beautiful natural elements like waterfalls. For instance, the Church Camp Trail, a moderate 3.5-mile out-and-back run, is known for its scenic waterfall, providing a refreshing highlight during your jog.
tips: One of the most outstanding limestone peaks in Durango belonging to the Urkiola Natural Park. Due to its geology and morphology, it is a well-known peak among the inhabitants of these lands, since it is perfectly visible from far away.
It is the highest summit of Lagarmendi although its neighbor Mugarra surpasses it in competition because its ascent is not so striking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, characteristic of a 'middle mountain route'. You'll encounter sections following old railway lines, which are generally flatter and well-established, ideal for jogging. Other parts traverse rural areas, forests, and fields, avoiding surfaced roads and occasionally crossing streams. While the overall average altitude is moderate, there are elevation changes throughout.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail?
faqs.answer: The route winds through the beautiful Urkiola Natural Park and passes through charming municipalities, showcasing medieval roads, old mills, and traditional farmhouses. You'll find spectacular panoramic views, especially from places like Garai, known as the 'balcony of Durangaldea', and from the vicinity of Mugarra Summit (969 m). Historical landmarks include the San Agustín de Etxebarria Church and the unique Berriozabaleta fountain. You'll also pass near Leungane Summit (1,008 m).

faqs.question: Is the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the full route is considered 'difficult' for jogging due to its length and elevation, the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail (GR 229) is generally rated as 'easy' to 'medium' for hikers. It's divided into five stages, with individual stage lengths varying from 8.5 km to 22.9 km. This suggests that sections of the trail are manageable for those with a reasonable fitness level, making it accessible for regular joggers or those looking for shorter, less strenuous segments.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll find parts of the Mikeldi Durangaldea (GR 229) itself, as well as segments of Erleabe, erleen bidea, Legarmendiko ibilbide handia, Ardoaren eta arrainaren bidea, Ikazkinen bidea, PR-BI 84, and Camino de Santa Lucia.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging, with lush landscapes in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. While winter jogging is possible, be prepared for colder temperatures and potentially wet or muddy conditions. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable during hotter months.

faqs.question: Is the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in the Durangaldea region, especially within the Urkiola Natural Park. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other trail users. Please remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or Urkiola Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail or to enter the Urkiola Natural Park. It is freely accessible to the public. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife or vegetation.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through various municipalities and rural areas within Durangaldea. While specific trailhead parking isn't detailed, you can generally find parking in the towns and villages along the route, such as Durango or other starting points for the trail's different stages. Look for public parking areas in these locations.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The trail offers spectacular panoramic views, particularly from areas near Garai, often called the 'balcony of Durangaldea'. You'll also get great vistas from the vicinity of Mugarra Summit (969 m). Beyond natural beauty, you'll encounter historical landmarks like the San Agustín de Etxebarria Church and the unique Berriozabaleta fountain.

faqs.question: What kind of natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through diverse natural settings, including sections of the Urkiola Natural Park. You'll jog through pine forests, open fields, and rural landscapes. Expect to see various flora and potentially local wildlife, especially in the quieter, more natural sections of the route. The trail also features streams and small waterfalls in certain areas.

faqs.question: Is the Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Mikeldi Durangaldea Trail (GR 229) is described as a circular route, meaning it forms a loop. This allows joggers to experience a continuous journey through the Durangaldea region without retracing their steps, offering a comprehensive exploration of its diverse landscapes and cultural heritage.
